extraction_notes: Many publishers (Wiley, Elsevier, Nature, ScienceDirect, MDPI direct) returned 403/404 in this batch. Where full-text was inaccessible, PubMed abstracts were used and only edges directly stated in the abstract were extracted. Highest yield came from: Beveridge 2023 JXB apical dominance (full review accessible), Rameau 2014 Frontiers (full text), Yang & Jiao 2016 Frontiers (full text), Coudert 2015 eLife (full text), Katyayini 2020 Frontiers (full text), Nguyen & Emery 2017 JXB (full text), de Jong 2019 PLoS Genetics (full text). Tanaka 2006, Stirnberg 2012, Dun 2009, Jones 2010 only abstracts retrieved.

edges.mechanism: Auxin represses cytokinin biosynthesis, reducing CK levels in the apical-dominant stem.

evidence.title: Lessons from a century of apical dominance research
evidence.evidence_sentence: Apically derived auxin (IAA) stimulates SL biosynthesis... reduces CK levels.
evidence.claim: Apical auxin reduces cytokinin levels in the stem

edges.mechanism: Auxin transcriptionally upregulates SL biosynthesis genes (CCD7/CCD8/MAX3/MAX4).

evidence.title: Lessons from a century of apical dominance research
evidence.evidence_sentence: Apically derived auxin (IAA) stimulates SL biosynthesis.

edges.mechanism: SL signaling upregulates BRC1 expression in axillary buds.

evidence.title: Lessons from a century of apical dominance research
evidence.evidence_sentence: SL up-regulates BRC1 expression.

edges.mechanism: BRC1 acts as a central inhibitor of bud release.

evidence.title: Lessons from a century of apical dominance research
evidence.evidence_sentence: BRC1 acts to inhibit bud release.

edges.mechanism: BRC1 upregulates ABA-responsive regulators (HB21/40/53) and ABA biosynthesis (NCED3).

evidence.title: Lessons from a century of apical dominance research
evidence.evidence_sentence: BRC1 up-regulates ABA levels and signalling in buds.
evidence.claim: BRC1 promotes ABA accumulation in buds

edges.mechanism: Sucrose downregulates SL biosynthesis pathway.

evidence.title: Lessons from a century of apical dominance research
evidence.evidence_sentence: promotes...IAA.

edges.mechanism: Tre6P levels reflect cellular sucrose status; high sucrose drives high T6P.

edges.mechanism: T6P is a positive sugar-status signal that enhances branching independently of BRC1.

evidence.evidence_sentence: Tre6P enhances shoot branching.

edges.mechanism: T6P binds and inactivates SnRK1 catalytic subunit, releasing growth repression.

evidence.title: Lessons from a century of apical dominance research
evidence.evidence_sentence: inactivated by Tre6P.

edges.mechanism: Removal of apex redirects sugar flux; sucrose rapidly accumulates in nodal stem and buds.

evidence.title: Lessons from a century of apical dominance research
evidence.evidence_sentence: Sugar levels increase rapidly after decapitation.

edges.mechanism: Elevated auxin in the bud increases gibberellin biosynthesis, supporting elongation.

evidence.title: Lessons from a century of apical dominance research
evidence.evidence_sentence: positive effect of auxin on GA content.

edges.mechanism: GA induces and sustains bud elongation after release.

evidence.title: Lessons from a century of apical dominance research
evidence.evidence_sentence: GA is highly effective at inducing sustained growth.

edges.mechanism: Phosphate sufficiency suppresses SL biosynthesis; deficiency strongly induces it.

evidence.title: Lessons from a century of apical dominance research
evidence.evidence_sentence: SL levels in roots... highly regulated by phosphate.

edges.mechanism: Nitrogen sufficiency suppresses SL biosynthesis; deficiency induces it.

evidence.title: Lessons from a century of apical dominance research
evidence.evidence_sentence: highly regulated by... nitrogen.

edges.mechanism: Cytokinin enhances PIN1 abundance/polarisation, increasing stem auxin transport capacity.

evidence.title: Lessons from a century of apical dominance research
evidence.evidence_sentence: CK stimulation... associated with PIN regulation and enhanced auxin transport.

edges.mechanism: Apex removal de-represses PsIPT1/IPT2 in the nodal stem, locally elevating cytokinin.

evidence.title: Auxin controls local cytokinin biosynthesis in the nodal stem in apical dominance
evidence.evidence_sentence: After removing the shoot apex, expression of the pea IPT genes increases substantially in the nodal stem alongside cytokinin accumulation.

edges.mechanism: IAA suppresses PsIPT1/IPT2 transcription in nodal stems.

evidence.title: Auxin controls local cytokinin biosynthesis in the nodal stem in apical dominance
evidence.evidence_sentence: When indole-3-acetic acid (auxin) is applied, this IPT expression becomes suppressed.
evidence.claim: Auxin represses IPT in nodal stem

edges.mechanism: IPT (isopentenyltransferase) catalyses the rate-limiting step of cytokinin biosynthesis.

evidence.title: Auxin controls local cytokinin biosynthesis in the nodal stem in apical dominance
evidence.evidence_sentence: PsIPT1 and PsIPT2 transcripts were undetectable; after decapitation, they were markedly induced in the nodal stem along with accumulation of CK.

edges.mechanism: ABA accumulates in dormant buds and inhibits initiation of outgrowth.

evidence.title: Is ABA the earliest upstream inhibitor of apical dominance?
evidence.evidence_sentence: ABA levels and associated signaling may play a much more prominent role in the initiation.

edges.mechanism: High R:FR (sun) reduces bud ABA within hours, releasing buds from ABA-mediated dormancy.

evidence.title: Is ABA the earliest upstream inhibitor of apical dominance?
evidence.evidence_sentence: as rapidly as 3 h after high R:FR treatment, there is a sustained decline of bud ABA.

edges.mechanism: ABA reduces IAA accumulation in the bud during dormancy.

evidence.title: Is ABA the earliest upstream inhibitor of apical dominance?
evidence.evidence_sentence: ABA... hindered the accumulation of IAA in the bud.

edges.mechanism: Active PHYB constrains ABA signalling; inactivation under low R:FR de-represses ABA.

evidence.title: Is ABA the earliest upstream inhibitor of apical dominance?
evidence.evidence_sentence: PHYB fails to constrain an ABA signal.

edges.mechanism: Bioactive GAs feedback-induce GA2ox catabolic enzymes (homeostatic loop) in dormant buds.

evidence.evidence_sentence: GA2oxs that irreversibly deactivate bioactive GAs by 2-beta-hydroxylation.

edges.mechanism: Apex removal rapidly downregulates GA2ox in axillary buds, allowing bioactive GA accumulation.

evidence.title: Dual Role of Gibberellin in Perennial Shoot Branching: Inhibition and Activation
evidence.evidence_sentence: decapitation significantly downregulated them within a few hours.

evidence.evidence_sentence: GA3ox2 was significantly upregulated between 12 and 24 h.

evidence.title: Dual Role of Gibberellin in Perennial Shoot Branching: Inhibition and Activation
evidence.evidence_sentence: GA3ox2-mediated de novo GA-biosynthesis is initiated between 12 and 24 h.

edges.mechanism: Nitrate sufficiency promotes shoot branching; nitrate-rich Arabidopsis plants produce more branches.

evidence.title: Natural variation in Arabidopsis shoot branching plasticity in response to nitrate supply affects fitness
evidence.evidence_sentence: Wild-type (Col) Arabidopsis plants grown under nitrate sufficient conditions produce more branches.

edges.mechanism: Plant nitrogen status modulates cytokinin biosynthesis (IPT3 in roots), with nitrate elevating CK.

evidence.title: Natural variation in Arabidopsis shoot branching plasticity in response to nitrate supply affects fitness
evidence.evidence_sentence: cytokinin levels can be modulated by nutrient supply.

edges.mechanism: MAX3 (SL biosynthesis) loss of function increases branching; MAX3 sits at branching plasticity QTL peak.

evidence.title: Natural variation in Arabidopsis shoot branching plasticity in response to nitrate supply affects fitness
evidence.evidence_sentence: MAX3 strigolactone biosynthetic gene is located near the peak of the QTL for shoot branching plasticity.

edges.mechanism: Apical-derived auxin globally suppresses bud outgrowth; this is an ancient mechanism conserved from moss to angiosperms.

evidence.title: Three ancient hormonal cues co-ordinate shoot branching in a moss
evidence.evidence_sentence: auxin can act as a global suppressor of branching.

edges.mechanism: SL biosynthesised at gametophore base locally suppresses branching (ancient role conserved across land plants).

evidence.title: Three ancient hormonal cues co-ordinate shoot branching in a moss
evidence.evidence_sentence: basal expression of a strigolactone biosynthetic gene can account for predicted basal inhibitor.

edges.mechanism: CKX (cytokinin oxidase/dehydrogenase) family members enzymatically degrade cytokinins; OE reduces CK levels.

evidence.title: Three ancient hormonal cues co-ordinate shoot branching in a moss
evidence.evidence_sentence: cytokinin degradation is increased, thereby decreasing cytokinin levels.

edges.mechanism: D14 receptor with MAX2 F-box recruits and ubiquitinates SMXL6/7/8 (D53 in rice) for proteasomal degradation upon SL perception.

evidence.title: Lessons from a century of apical dominance research
evidence.evidence_sentence: targeting the rice DWARF53 (D53) or the Arabidopsis SMXL6/7/8 for ubiquitination.

edges.mechanism: SMXL6/7/8 recruit TPL/TPR co-repressors to repress BRC1 transcription; their degradation by SL releases BRC1.

evidence.title: Lessons from a century of apical dominance research
evidence.evidence_sentence: recruit TPL co-repressors to repress the transcription of BRC1.

edges.mechanism: MAX2 F-box subunit of SCF complex targets SMXL6/7/8 for proteasomal degradation in concert with D14.

evidence.title: Multiple pathways regulate shoot branching
evidence.evidence_sentence: targeting proteins to the proteasome for degradation.

edges.mechanism: SL is hydrolysed and bound by the D14 alpha/beta-hydrolase receptor, which then interacts with MAX2.

evidence.title: Multiple pathways regulate shoot branching
evidence.evidence_sentence: SL-receptor... interacts with the F-box protein MAX2.

literature_gaps_observed: PYR/PYL/RCAR ABA receptor specifics in shoot branching not addressed in any accessible 2017-2024 review of this batch.

CYP707A (ABA catabolism) family role in bud release: still implied rather than demonstrated; Nguyen & Emery 2017 references high R:FR-induced ABA decline but does not name CYP707A as the responsible enzyme.
AHP (Arabidopsis Histidine Phosphotransfer) intermediates between AHK receptors and ARR TFs not detailed in the accessible reviews.
Type-A vs Type-B ARR distinct roles in axillary bud outgrowth (vs AM initiation) underspecified.
LOG (LONELY GUY) family role in activating cytokinin in axillary buds not covered in this batch.
GA20ox biosynthesis specifically in branching context (vs internode elongation) not fully resolved.
PIF4/PIF5 role in branching beyond hypocotyl elongation: gap in cited literature for this batch.
CO/GI/FKF1/CDF1 photoperiod cascade: Rameau 2014 mentions photoperiod-flowering linkage but does not directly couple CO to branching outside FT.
SnRK1 catalytic subunit (SnRK1.1/KIN10) downstream targets in buds: only mentioned at the T6P-SnRK1 interaction level.
TPS1 (T6P synthase) role in branching specifically (vs TPS family at large) not detailed in accessible content.

Many primary papers (Tanaka 2006, Stirnberg 2012, Dun 2009, Jones 2010) inaccessible due to publisher paywalls; only abstracts retrieved. A targeted re-attempt via institutional proxy may yield additional edges from Methods/Results sections.
